name: Ledge Grab
description: A platforming recovery state that detects a ledge, validates clearance, snaps the character to a hang point, and waits for climb, drop, or jump input.
design_notes: Ledge grab requires reliable ledge detection and failure recovery. Define ledge_id, grab point, snap position, top clearance, blocked ledges, stamina or grip rules, camera behavior, and debug visualization. It should save intended near-misses without grabbing decorative edges or stealing control from other traversal actions.
design_purpose: Give players a forgiving recovery when they almost reach a platform edge.
Support vertical level design with readable ledges, drops, and climb routes.
Create a state bridge between jump, wall movement, climb, mantle, and fall recovery.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can catch the edge at the last moment and choose how to recover.

parameters.name: ledge_grab_probe_height_units
parameters.description: Vertical probe span used to find candidate ledges.

parameters.name: ledge_grab_forward_probe_units
parameters.description: Forward probe distance from character to ledge face.

parameters.name: ledge_snap_position_offset
parameters.description: Offset applied when aligning character to hang position.

parameters.name: ledge_clearance_check_id
parameters.description: Clearance rule used before allowing hang or climb.

parameters.name: ledge_grab_input_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Buffered grab or jump input accepted near ledge contact.

parameters.name: ledge_grip_stamina_units
parameters.description: Optional stamina resource available while hanging.

edge_cases: The ledge is detected but top clearance is blocked by a moving crate, enemy, or closing door.
A player can approach the same edge from jump, wall slide, climb, or knockback states with different priorities.
Moving platforms need ledge_id and snap position stored relative to platform transform.
Networked ledge grabs require server validation of ledge id and character position correction.
common_bugs: Detection rays catch decorative trim that has no safe hang or climb position.
Character snaps through the ledge because clearance is checked after changing position.
Hang state disables gravity but never restores it when drop input is buffered.
Camera recenters behind the wall and hides climb or drop options.
balancing_notes: Automatic ledge grab increases forgiveness but can interrupt intentional falls or speed routes.
Grip stamina adds tension but should not punish players while they are reading the next safe input.
Ledge detection tolerance should match art language so players learn which edges are valid.
accessibility_notes: Provide optional auto-grab, input buffering, and visible ledge affordances for precision-heavy sections.
Avoid making required ledges distinguishable only by tiny geometry differences.

implementation_notes.unity: Use multiple raycasts or shape casts for ledge face, top surface, and clearance before committing ledge_id. Store snap offset, disable ordinary movement through platforming_state_id, and draw debug probes for designers.
implementation_notes.godot: Use RayCast or ShapeCast nodes for forward and top checks, then validate clearance before snapping. Keep ledge state on CharacterBody and restore gravity/input through a single drop or climb transition.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use trace channels or ledge components to find hang points. Server should validate ledge_id and snap transform, while animation montages read the committed hang state rather than driving detection.
implementation_notes.web: Store ledge candidates from deterministic collision probes in the fixed step. Commit hang state only after clearance passes, then render hang animation from the stored snap position.

relationships.reason: Ledge grab usually provides the snap point and hang state that ledge climb resolves into a top-out state.
Long ledge hangs can spend stamina to prevent indefinite safety on climbable edges.
Automatic mantling and manual ledge grab need priority rules when the same ledge is detected.

example_games.title: Tomb Raider
example_games.note: Uses ledge grabs and hangs as a major traversal layer.

example_games.title: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time
example_games.note: Uses ledge grabs to connect jumps and climbing routes.
